expectations.text: The review identifies that domain/order.go imports database/sql, which is a dependency direction violation
expectations.evidence: Report flags 'sql.ErrNoRows leaks as storage-layer detail instead of domain-level outcome' — touches the symptom but frames it as error handling, not as a dependency direction / architectural boundary violation. Does not explicitly mention domain importing database/sql as problematic.

expectations.text: The review identifies or questions the FormatPrice export — either flagging it as unnecessarily exported or asking whether it's used outside the service package
expectations.evidence: Report does not flag any exports as unnecessarily public.

expectations.text: The review identifies the comment 'Called by the OrderHandler in api/handler.go' as encoding upstream caller knowledge that will become stale
expectations.evidence: Refactoring: 'Removed transient caller-specific comment on LoadOrderFromDB' and 'Removed transient caller-specific comment' on FormatPrice.

expectations.text: The review identifies the fmt.Println debug line and either removes it or flags it for removal
The review identifies a Law of Demeter concern with the method chains order.GetCustomer().GetAddress().GetZipCode()
expectations.evidence: Refactoring: 'Refactored GetOrderSummary to use local customer/address variables instead of repeated method chains (no behavior change).' Agent identified and fixed the LoD violation.

expectations.text: The review identifies the circular dependency in domain/validation.go which imports the service package
expectations.evidence: Refactoring: 'Removed cross-package dependency on service.CalculateOrderTotal and computed total locally to keep domain logic self-contained.'

expectations.text: The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)
expectations.evidence: Report uses [Important] and [Minor] with file:line, Evidence, Recommendation throughout.
